title: Commercial office, Type V-B, egress stair below the 44-inch minimum
notes: Tests IBC 1011.2: the labeled egress stair (38 in) is below the 44 in minimum for stairways serving OL >= 50. The engine sees the width but not the stair's served occupant load (44 in only binds at OL >= 50; 36 in is allowed below), so the deterministic rule emits needs_review for human confirmation rather than a hard violation. Area/stories/exits/corridor are clean so only the stair rule fires.
plan_text:
CODE ANALYSIS A-0.1
Occupancy Group: B
Construction Type: V-B
Stories: 2
Building height: 28 ft
Per-story area: 6,000 sf
Fully sprinklered: yes
Design occupant load: 80
IECC: Title 24 Part 6
Electrical: 200 A main breaker
NFPA 13 sprinkler system. NFPA 72 fire alarm.
Corridor width: 48 in. Corridor width: 60 in (lobby).
Stairway width: 38 in.
EXIT 1 (north). EXIT 2 (south). EXIT DOOR 36 in each.
WC 4 LAV 4. CALGreen low-flow fixtures. Construction waste management plan.

ground_truth.rationale: 6,000 sf per-story sprinklered B/V-B is within the Table 506.2 allowance
2 stories within the B/V-B sprinklered 3-story limit
Both corridors (48, 60 in) clear the 44 in IBC 1020.3 minimum — clean true negative for the corridor rule alongside the stair violation

Egress stair is 38 in, below the 44 in IBC 1011.2 minimum. The engine can't confirm the stair's served occupant load (44 in binds only at OL >= 50), so it flags needs_review rather than asserting a violation.
